title: LangChain
purpose: Orchestrate model calls, tools and state into inspectable AI workflows.
outcomes: Explain LangChain concepts from first principles
Choose when to use and when to avoid important LangChain features
Implement, test and debug practical LangChain solutions
Answer conceptual, internal-working and production interview questions

stages.title: Fundamentals
stages.objective: Build an accurate mental model of LangChain before relying on frameworks or memorized answers.

topics.title: Messages, models and tools
topics.definition: An AI application combines a probabilistic model with deterministic context, tools, policies and evaluation. Retrieval supplies evidence; tool calls perform bounded actions; orchestration controls state and flow.
topics.whyItMatters: A language model alone may produce fluent but unsupported output and cannot safely access current private data or take actions without explicit controls.
whenToUse: Use prompting for instruction and output shaping
Use RAG when answers must be grounded in changing documents
Use tools or agents only when the task needs external information or action
whenNotToUse: Using an autonomous loop for a deterministic workflow
Treating retrieved text as trusted instructions
Shipping without a representative evaluation set
internalWorking: Input is tokenized and the model predicts successive token distributions
Retrieval embeds or searches a query and returns evidence chunks
An orchestrator builds context, invokes the model, validates output and optionally executes approved tools
Traces and evaluators measure quality, safety, latency and cost
tradeoffs: More context may improve recall but increase noise, latency and cost
Agents are flexible but less predictable than workflows
Stricter output validation improves reliability but needs fallback handling
commonMistakes: Evaluating only a few happy-path examples
topics.practicalExample: Retrieve authorized study chapters, rerank them, ask the model to answer with citations, reject unsupported claims and route low-confidence answers to a normal search or human review path.

topics.title: Loop limits
topics.definition: Object-oriented programming groups state with the behavior that protects and transforms it. Encapsulation protects invariants, polymorphism lets callers depend on contracts, and inheritance is one possible reuse mechanism.
topics.whyItMatters: It helps model long-lived domain concepts whose state must remain valid while implementations evolve behind stable interfaces.
whenToUse: Model entities such as Student, Assessment and Submission with clear invariants
Hide changeable implementation details behind an interface
Use polymorphism when several implementations honor the same behavioral contract
whenNotToUse: Creating classes that only hold unrelated utility functions
Using inheritance only to reuse code
Building deep hierarchies where composition expresses the relationship more clearly
internalWorking: An object has identity, state and behavior exposed through methods
A class defines construction and method dispatch rules
Dynamic dispatch selects an overriding implementation using the runtime object type
Encapsulation keeps invalid updates behind methods that enforce invariants
tradeoffs: Stable boundaries versus extra abstractions
Inheritance offers substitution but tightly couples parent and child
Mutable objects are convenient but increase coordination and testing cost
commonMistakes: Calling fields private without actually protecting invariants
Using instanceof chains instead of polymorphic behavior
topics.practicalExample: Assessment.submit(answer) can reject submissions after the deadline. The caller cannot directly mutate the internal status and bypass that rule.

interviewQuestions.question: What is State management?
interviewQuestions.answer: An AI application combines a probabilistic model with deterministic context, tools, policies and evaluation. Retrieval supplies evidence; tool calls perform bounded actions; orchestration controls state and flow.
followUps: What changes when the input or traffic for State management grows ten times?

interviewQuestions.question: What problem does State management solve?
interviewQuestions.answer: A language model alone may produce fluent but unsupported output and cannot safely access current private data or take actions without explicit controls.
followUps: Which failure is most likely in a production LangChain implementation?

interviewQuestions.question: When should you use State management?
interviewQuestions.answer: Use prompting for instruction and output shaping Use RAG when answers must be grounded in changing documents Use tools or agents only when the task needs external information or action
followUps: When would you choose a simpler alternative to State management?

interviewQuestions.question: When should you avoid State management?
interviewQuestions.answer: Using an autonomous loop for a deterministic workflow Treating retrieved text as trusted instructions Shipping without a representative evaluation set
followUps: What simpler alternative could replace State management, and what would be lost?

interviewQuestions.question: How does State management work internally?
interviewQuestions.answer: Input is tokenized and the model predicts successive token distributions Retrieval embeds or searches a query and returns evidence chunks An orchestrator builds context, invokes the model, validates output and optionally executes approved tools Traces and evaluators measure quality, safety, latency and cost
followUps: How would you test and monitor State management?

interviewQuestions.question: What are the main tradeoffs of State management?
interviewQuestions.answer: More context may improve recall but increase noise, latency and cost Agents are flexible but less predictable than workflows Stricter output validation improves reliability but needs fallback handling
followUps: Which tradeoff matters most under production load?

interviewQuestions.question: Give an interview-ready explanation of State management.
interviewQuestions.answer: I treat the model as one probabilistic component. For knowledge tasks I retrieve scoped evidence, separate data from instructions, require structured output and citations, and evaluate faithfulness. For tools I apply least privilege, approval gates, loop limits and complete tracing.
